_comment: Persisted GUI defaults. Each entry stores the startup value and a short explanation of its role.

add_noise._comment: Whether Poisson counting noise is added to the simulated detector data.

analysis_method._comment: Default analysis method used to recover size and polydispersity from the simulated data. Allowed values: 'NNLS', 'Tomchuk', 'Tenor'.

binning_mode._comment: Whether the 1D q bins are spaced linearly or logarithmically. Allowed values: 'Linear', 'Logarithmic'.

dist_type._comment: Probability distribution family used to generate the ensemble. Allowed values: 'Gaussian', 'Lognormal', 'Schulz', 'Boltzmann', 'Triangular', 'Uniform'.

ensemble_members._comment: Number of discrete ensemble members used when finite ensemble sampling is selected.

ensemble_sampling._comment: Whether the ensemble is integrated continuously or approximated with a finite number of members. Allowed values: 'Continuous', 'Discrete'.

flux_exp._comment: Base-10 exponent used in scientific notation for the central-pixel flux target.

flux_pre._comment: Mantissa used in scientific notation for the central-pixel flux target.

form_factor_model._comment: Forward model used for the particle form factor. Allowed values: 'Exact Sphere', 'Guinier Curvature', 'Exact Gaussian Chain', 'Exact Shell', 'Exact Thin Rod', 'Exact Thin Disk'.

mean_rg._comment: Mean radius of gyration of the simulated ensemble, in the app's working length units.

n_bins._comment: Number of q bins used when reducing the detector image to a 1D intensity profile.

nnls_basis_count._comment: Number of basis functions used in the NNLS inversion grid.

nnls_max_rg._comment: Largest radius of gyration included in the NNLS basis grid.

nnls_smooth_sigma._comment: Gaussian smoothing width applied to the recovered NNLS distribution.

optimal_flux._comment: Whether the app should override the user flux and choose an internally optimized flux value.

p_val._comment: Polydispersity parameter used to define the width of the selected size distribution.

phi2._comment: Second-order correction coefficient used by the Guinier-curvature forward model.

phi3._comment: Third-order correction coefficient used by the Guinier-curvature forward model.

pixel_size_um._comment: Physical detector pixel dimension in micrometers. The total detector width is pixels multiplied by this value.

pixels._comment: Number of detector pixels along one image dimension for the simulated 2D pattern.

q_max._comment: Maximum q value kept in the 1D profile and downstream analysis. The effective value is clipped to the instrument-derived detector q limit.

q_min._comment: Minimum q value included in the generated or analyzed 1D profile.

q_samples._comment: Number of q samples used internally when evaluating model form factors before detector binning.

radius_samples._comment: Number of radius or Rg support points used to build continuous ensemble distributions.

sample_detector_distance_cm._comment: Sample-to-detector distance used to convert detector position into q, in centimeters.

sim_mode._comment: Top-level simulation family used by the GUI and analysis pipeline. Allowed values: 'Polydisperse Spheres', 'Polydisperse IDP'.

smearing_x._comment: Gaussian detector smearing width along the horizontal detector axis, in pixels.

smearing_y._comment: Gaussian detector smearing width along the vertical detector axis, in pixels.

tenor_calibration_p_count._comment: Number of p values sampled in the Tenor-SAXS simulation calibration grid.

tenor_calibration_p_max._comment: Maximum p value included in the Tenor-SAXS simulation calibration grid.

tenor_calibration_p_min._comment: Minimum p value included in the Tenor-SAXS simulation calibration grid.

tenor_guinier_bins._comment: Number of radial bins used when estimating the apparent Guinier radius for Tenor-SAXS.

tenor_psf_count._comment: Number of PSF quartets scanned when searching for a stable Tenor-SAXS observable extraction.

tenor_psf_secondary_ratio._comment: Relative width factor used to define the secondary PSF in each Tenor-SAXS quartet.

tenor_psf_sigma_step._comment: Increment applied between successive Tenor-SAXS PSF scan candidates, in pixels.

tenor_psf_sigma_x_start._comment: Starting horizontal PSF sigma for the Tenor-SAXS PSF scan, in pixels.

tenor_psf_sigma_y_start._comment: Starting vertical PSF sigma for the Tenor-SAXS PSF scan, in pixels.

tenor_psf_truncate._comment: Gaussian kernel truncation radius, in sigma units, for digitally applied Tenor PSFs.

tenor_qrg_limit._comment: Upper qRg cutoff used when selecting the apparent Guinier fitting region for Tenor-SAXS.

tenor_radial_bins._comment: Number of radial sectors used in the Tenor-SAXS observable extraction workflow.

tenor_use_g3._comment: Whether the Tenor-SAXS G(Q) fit includes the cubic-in-Q term used in the MATLAB workflow.

tenor_use_m3._comment: Whether the Tenor-SAXS M(Q) fit includes the cubic-in-Q term used in the MATLAB workflow.

tomchuk_target_abs_error._comment: Target absolute fitting tolerance used by the Tomchuk recovery procedure.

wavelength_nm._comment: Beam wavelength used to convert detector position into q, in nanometers.
